zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Anne Elizabeth Standish於1480年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾阿斯普爾時，她的父親Christopher Standish Sir, Lord of Duxbury Manor, 14th ggf20歲，母親Alice Poole20歲。她於1500年與Ralph or Rauf Goodloe 13th ggf在英國英格蘭藍卡爾阿斯普爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1515年在她的家鄉享年35歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Anne Elizabeth Standish于1480年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾阿斯普爾时，她的父亲Christopher Standish Sir, Lord of Duxbury Manor, 14th ggf20岁，母亲Alice Poole20岁。她于1500年与Ralph or Rauf Goodloe 13th ggf在英国英格兰藍卡爾阿斯普爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1515年在她的家乡享年35岁。

nameMeanings.name: Standish
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Standish (Lancashire, now part of Greater Manchester), from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + edisc ‘enclosure, enclosed park’. Standish (Gloucestershire) does not appear to have contributed towards the surname.

History: Myles Standish (c. 1584–1656) was a soldier of fortune, from 1620 captain of the Mayflower Pilgrims at Plymouth Colony. Little is known of his origins and early life, but in his will he claimed to be descended from a leading Catholic family, the Standishes of Standish, Lancashire, England. He also claimed to have been deprived of his inheritance, a claim not confirmed.

variants.name: Standage
meanings: in Leicestershire and Buckinghamshire, the name may alternatively be from Stanedge Grange in Hartington Nether Quarter or Stanage End in Outseats (both Derbyshire); but evidence is needed for this. The placenames derive from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + ecg ‘edge, escarpment’.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Anne Elizabeth Standish was born in 1480, in Aspull, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Christopher Standish Sir, Lord of Duxbury Manor, 14th ggf, was 20 and her mother, Alice Poole, was 20. She married Ralph or Rauf Goodloe 13th ggf in 1500, in Aspull, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1515, in her hometown, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Anne Elizabeth Standish est née en 1480, à Aspull, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Christopher Standish Sir, Lord of Duxbury Manor, 14th ggf, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Alice Poole, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Ralph or Rauf Goodloe 13th ggf en 1500, à Aspull, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1515, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 35 ans.
